original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what issue was resolved in VRF Bug 364713?
original_answer: VRF (Bug ID 364713): Symptom: FQDN radius-server does not resolve to the respective Host IP and tracking flags it as unreachable. Scenario: This issue may occur when RADIUS servers are configured using FQDN hostnames rather than IP addresses and RADIUS tracking is enabled after a switch reboot. Workaround: Remove and re-add the RADIUS server configuration after each reboot. Restart the radius tracking process after each reboot. Configure static DNS host entries for the RADIUS servers in the switch configuration. Use IP addresses instead of FQDN hostnames for RADIUS servers. Force restart radius-srv-trkd process for the quick fix.

reason: The original answer includes unnecessary details and a workaround that is not directly related to the Boot Process issue.
original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what issue was resolved in Boot Process Bug 319610?
original_answer: Boot Process (Bug ID 319610): Symptom: When switching between IPTV channels, there is an intermittent delay of 2-6 seconds before the new video stream is displayed to the end user. Scenario: The system experiences a small delay in the program of a new multicast flow after receiving the IGMP report. During this time, critical initial video packets containing SPS (Sequence Parameter Set) and PPS (Picture Parameter Set) information may be dropped. These packets are essential for the decoder to properly display the H.264 video stream. Workaround: Configure static IGMP groups for the multicast streams as follows: static igmp join <multicast-group-address> interface <decoder-port> This ensures the hardware is pre-programmed with the multicast flows, eliminating the join latency. configure interface IP and Active gateway IP/MAC when interface is UP and running.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what issue was resolved in SNMP Bug 367181?
original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 367181): Symptom: Switches may fail to respond to SNMP queries after upgrading from 10.08 version of code to 10.13 code. Scenario: This issue occurs when a switch has been configured with snmp-server response-source prior to upgrade. Workaround: After upgrading, access the switch shell and either: 1. Delete the file /fs/nos/net-snmp/agent-swns/snmp.conf, or 2. Edit the file to comment out (with #) the line containing [snmp] srcipaddr entry 3. Restart the SNMP service with systemctl restart snmpd-wrapper-in-c.service.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what issue was resolved in RADIUS Bug 363074?
original_answer: RADIUS (Bug ID 363074): Symptom: The switch intermittently fails to include the Framed-IP attribute in RADIUS accounting packets. This results in missing IP address information for authenticated clients. Scenario: The issue is observed when a client disconnects and reconnects after a short interval (for example, 90 seconds). The Framed-IP attribute may be missing even though the client has a valid IP address and is visible in other system outputs. Workaround: Ensure continuous client traffic after reconnection to allow the switch to relearn the IP address. Alternatively, re-enable MAC authentication on the port or perform a shut/no shut cycle to restore expected behavior.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what known issue is documented for NTP Bug 322650?
original_answer: NTP (Bug ID 322650): Symptom: NTP entries are not updating correctly with dhcpv4 and v6 client enable/disable Scenario 1: When DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the same VLAN with DHCPv4 enabled and DHCPv6 disabled: Disabling DHCPv4 and enabling DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected Disabling both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected When both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the same VLAN with DHCPv4 disabled and DHCPv6 enabled: Enabling DHCPv4 and disabling DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected Scenario 2: When DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 clients are on the different VLANs: Re-enabling DHCP mode on lower interface VLAN does not update the NTP entries as expected Disabling both DHCPv4 and DHCPv6 does not update the NTP entries as expected.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade?
original_answer: Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, PL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, how do you perform the software upgrade?
original_answer: Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method. 2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue. 3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time. 4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
cleaned_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, how do you perform the software upgrade?
cleaned_answer:
Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow these steps in order:
1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.
2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any device updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.
3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

cleaned_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what are the steps to perform the software upgrade?

Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow these steps in order:

2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any device updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.
3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1040, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade?
original_answer: Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, PL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what caveat is documented for SNMP?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what issue was resolved in NTP Bug 371717?
original_answer: NTP (Bug ID 371717): Symptom: Excessive connection lost and connection established logging when multiple NTP servers are configured. Scenario: If the NTP server changes, a connection lost and connection established is logged to the event log. Workaround: The fix disables logging of normal server changes and only logs events if server reachability reaches 0 or NTP synchronization is lost.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade?
original_answer: Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, PL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1060, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade?
original_answer: Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, PL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1060, how do you perform the software upgrade?
original_answer: Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method. 2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue. 3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time. 4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
cleaned_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.15.1060, how do you perform the software upgrade?
cleaned_answer:
Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow these steps in order:
1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.
2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any device updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.
3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.0002, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what issue was resolved in Logging Bug 358847?
original_answer: Logging (Bug ID 358847): Symptom: A VSF stack member switch unexpectedly reboots due to the ops-switchd process crashing with a signal: 6 error. Scenario: In some scenarios with excessive ACL event logging, a crash may occur due to a failure in the system when processing a high volume of ACL log entries. Workaround: Minimize the use of the log keyword in ACL entries, especially on high-traffic rules. Consider using ACL counters instead of logging for monitoring traffic patterns.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what issue was resolved in EVPN Bug 364576?
original_answer: EVPN (Bug ID 364576): Symptom: Missing ARP entries showing as "incomplete" in the ARP table, causing connectivity issues in remote VTEP. Scenario: This issue may occur in an EVPN VXLAN environment with VSX pairs when a VIP (Virtual IP) moves from one node to another node, MAC address aging occurs or after executing "clear mac-address" on the switch. original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade?
original_answer: Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, PL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, how do you perform the software upgrade?
original_answer: Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method. 2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue. 3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time. 4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1010, what issue was resolved in NTP Bug 371717?
original_answer: NTP (Bug ID 371717): Symptom: Excessive connection lost and connection established logging when multiple NTP servers are configured. Scenario: If the NTP server changes, a connection lost and connection established is logged to the event log. Workaround: The fix disables logging of normal server changes and only logs events if server reachability reaches 0 or NTP synchronization is lost.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1010, what issue was resolved in OVSDB Bug 386032?
original_answer: OVSDB (Bug ID 386032): Symptom: Switches may encounter service disruptions or become unresponsive, leading to connectivity loss or requiring reconnection to Central. original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1010, what issue was resolved in PVST Bug 389395?
original_answer: PVST (Bug ID 389395): Symptom: An STP port incorrectly transitions into Disabled/Blocked state. Scenario: During certain port flap events, STP may erroneously mark a port as Disabled/Blocked, even when the link should remain active. Workaround: Shut down all affected ports. Restart the hpe-pvstd process using the following commands: start-shell sudo bash ps aux | grep hpe-pvstd | grep -v grep systemctl restart hpe-pvstd ps aux | grep hpe-pvstd | grep -v grep exit exit

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1010, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade?
original_answer: Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, PL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1010, how do you perform the software upgrade?
original_answer: Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method. 2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue. 3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time. 4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
cleaned_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1010, how do you perform the software upgrade?
cleaned_answer:
Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow these steps in order:
1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.
2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any device updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.
3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what issue was resolved in SNMP Bug 400261?
original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 400261): Symptom: SNMP OID sysUpTimeInstance (1.3.6.1.2.1.1.3.0) may return an incorrect value when polled, leading to inaccurate uptime reporting in monitoring systems. Scenario: This issue occurs on switches running AOS-CX 10.16 when using SNMP to query sysUpTimeInstance. The reported value does not match the actual system uptime, causing discrepancies in monitoring tools. Workaround: Use the OID hrSystemUptime (1.3.6.1.2.1.25.1.1.0) instead of sysUpTimeInstance for accurate system uptime reporting.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade?
original_answer: Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, PL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, how do you perform the software upgrade?
original_answer: Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method. 2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue. 3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time. 4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
cleaned_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, how do you perform the software upgrade?
cleaned_answer:
Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow these steps in order:
1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.
2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any device updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.
3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1030, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1030, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade?
original_answer: Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, PL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1030, how do you perform the software upgrade?
original_answer: Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method. 2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue. 3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time. 4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
cleaned_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1030, how do you perform the software upgrade?

1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.

3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what limitation is mentioned for SNMP?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - Overlay/system partition information is included in HOST-RESOURCES-MIB polling. Monitoring tools should not treat this as an error.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what issue was resolved in Mirroring Bug 410596?
original_answer: Mirroring (Bug ID 410596): Symptom: ERSPAN mirroring on 6100/4100i switches fails to capture TX (egress) traffic from remote switches. Only RX traffic and locally-originated TX traffic are mirrored to the ERSPAN destination. ICMP requests from remote switches are missing in the mirrored GRE traffic, while replies are captured correctly. Scenario: This issue occurs when ERSPAN mirroring is configured on a 6100 or 4100i switch and the GRE tunnel egress port is the same as the port where the original packet ingressed. For example, when traffic arrives on port 1/1/3 from a remote switch and needs to be mirrored via GRE tunnel that also exits through port 1/1/3, the mirror fails due to ingress port filtering. Workaround: Connect the ERSPAN tunnel destination (mirror endpoint) via a separate physical port than the mirrored source uplink port, ensuring the tunnel traffic exits through a different port than where the original packet was received.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what issue was resolved in MAC Tables Bug 415095?
original_answer: MAC Tables (Bug ID 415095): Symptom: MACs are only showing on one side of a VSX pair. For instance, a MAC is learned on a physical port on one VSX device and is missing from the peer VSX device. Scenario: This issue can occur after resolving a network loop which causes the MACs to quickly move from one VSX device to the other, particularly when MACs are moving from EVPN tunnels to physical ports. Workaround: Using the CLI to clear the problematic MAC address.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what known issue is documented for PBR Bug 407418?
original_answer: PBR (Bug ID 407418): Symptom: Classifier Policy is not enforced for traffic coming from an ISL link. Scenario: For a topology with VSX+ VRRP, if the policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting classifier policy enforced. Even though the policy action is PBR redirect, those packets are not getting redirected. Workaround: Two workarounds are possible to prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL. 1. Use active gateway to give better active active redundancy. Packets reaching either VSX device process the packet destined to active gateway. 2. Turn off the interface connection from VRRP secondary device to down switch. This method may cause redundancy and is not preferred.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade?
original_answer: Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, PL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, how do you perform the software upgrade?
original_answer: Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method. 2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue. 3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time. 4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
cleaned_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, how do you perform the software upgrade?
cleaned_answer:
Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow these steps in order:
1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.
2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any device updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.
3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what issue was resolved in Device Finger Printing Bug 386320?
original_answer: Device Finger Printing (Bug ID 386320): Symptom: Customers see frequent log entries: HTTP <MAC-ADDRESS> - Invalid packet length or headers during normal HTTP traffic, even when no real packet corruption exists. No functional impact; just log noise. Scenario: This issue occurs on legitimate TCP packets to port 80 with no payload (ACK, SYN/ACK, FIN, keep-alive, window updates). Previously these were funneled into an error path (or indistinguishable from malformed cases), producing repeated ERR logs under moderate web or update traffic. Workaround: Disable the HTTP user-agent in device fingerprinting configuration.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what issue was resolved in REST Bug 393894?
original_answer: REST (Bug ID 393894): Symptom: Notification clients stop receiving updates, sessions drop, a core dump is recorded, and the REST service restarts. Workaround: Wait for the REST service to complete its automatic restart, then reconnect the notification clients and reapply the desired subscriptions.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade?
original_answer: Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, PL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, how do you perform the software upgrade?
original_answer: Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method. 2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue. 3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time. 4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
cleaned_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, how do you perform the software upgrade?
cleaned_answer:
Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow these steps in order:
1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.
2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any device updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.
3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what issue was resolved in Central connectivity Bug 392076?
original_answer: Central connectivity (Bug ID 392076): Symptom: Switch is unable to connect to Central. Scenario: This issue may occur when attempting to onboard switches that are connected to Central. The process connecting to YANG is creating more sockets than expected, leading to overload of the yang-resolverd server and subsequent disconnections. Workaround: Restart yang-resolverd daemon to reestablish the operation of the daemon. Restart hpe-restd daemon to make a clean connection to YANG after restarting the yang-resolverd daemon.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what issue was resolved in Device Profile Bug 407591?
original_answer: Device Profile (Bug ID 407591): Symptom: Clients are unable to onboard on to a LAG interface. original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what issue was resolved in SNMP Bug 408309?
original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 408309): Symptom: SNMPv3 user password changes do not take effect, after deleting and re-creating the user with a new password, the device still accepts the old password and rejects the new one. original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade?
original_answer: Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, PL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, how do you perform the software upgrade?
original_answer: Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method. 2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue. 3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time. 4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
cleaned_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, how do you perform the software upgrade?
cleaned_answer:
Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow these steps in order:
1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.
2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any device updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.
3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for SNMP?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what limitation is mentioned for SNMP?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - Overlay/system partition information is included in HOST-RESOURCES-MIB polling. The overlay partition is a read-only partition and this is reflected in SNMP queries. hrFSAccess.3 = readOnly(2) is expected behavior for overlay partitions. Monitoring tools should not treat this as an error.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what issue was resolved in Mirroring Bug 410596?
original_answer: Mirroring (Bug ID 410596): Symptom: ERSPAN mirroring on 6100/4100i switches fails to capture TX (egress) traffic from remote switches. Only RX traffic and locally-originated TX traffic are mirrored to the ERSPAN destination. ICMP requests from remote switches are missing in the mirrored GRE traffic, while replies are captured correctly. Scenario: This issue occurs when ERSPAN mirroring is configured on a 6100 or 4100i switch and the GRE tunnel egress port is the same as the port where the original packet ingressed. For example, when traffic arrives on port 1/1/3 from a remote switch and needs to be mirrored via GRE tunnel that also exits through port 1/1/3, the mirror fails due to ingress port filtering. Workaround: Connect the ERSPAN tunnel destination (mirror endpoint) via a separate physical port than the mirrored source uplink port, ensuring the tunnel traffic exits through a different port than where the original packet was received.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what issue was resolved in MAC Tables Bug 415095?
original_answer: MAC Tables (Bug ID 415095): Symptom: MACs are only showing on one side of a VSX pair. For instance, a MAC is learned on a physical port on one VSX device and is missing from the peer VSX device. Scenario: This issue can occur after resolving a network loop which causes the MACs to quickly move from one VSX device to the other, particularly when MACs are moving from EVPN tunnels to physical ports. Workaround: Using the CLI to clear the problematic MAC address.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what known issue is documented for PBR Bug 407418?
original_answer: PBR (Bug ID 407418): Symptom: Classifier Policy is not enforced for traffic coming from an ISL link. Scenario: For a topology with VSX+ VRRP, if the policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting classifier policy enforced. Even though the policy action is PBR redirect, those packets are not getting redirected. Workaround: Two workarounds are possible to prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL. 1. Use active gateway to give better active active redundancy. Packets reaching either VSX device process the packet destined to active gateway. 2. Turn off the interface connection from VRRP secondary device to down switch. This method may cause redundancy and is not preferred.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade?
original_answer: Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, PL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, how do you perform the software upgrade?
original_answer: Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method. 2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue. 3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time. 4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
cleaned_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, how do you perform the software upgrade?
cleaned_answer:
Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow these steps in order:
1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.
2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any device updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.
3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.18.0001, what issue was resolved in Internal Services Bug 407591?
original_answer: Internal Services (Bug ID 407591): Symptom: Dual-port AP uplinks connected via a LAG intermittently show member interfaces as “Blocked by Port Access Security.” the LAG stops forwarding and the end device cannot onboard until the interfaces are toggled. Scenario: This issue may occur when port-access device-profile is enabled on the LAG with mode block-until-profile-applied, using LLDP for profiling. original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.18.0001, what issue was resolved in DHCP Relay Bug 423594?
original_answer: DHCP Relay (Bug ID 423594): Symptom: DHCP clients connected to a switch configured as a DHCP relay agent may fail to obtain an IP address when DHCPv4 Snooping and DHCPv4 Relay are both enabled on the same VLAN. DHCP clients that perform explicit source MAC address validation may drop the relayed DHCP response packets. Scenario: This issue occurs when both DHCPv4 Relay and DHCPv4 Snooping are enabled on the same VLAN. In this configuration, DHCP server responses (Offer/Ack) are forwarded to the client with an invalid (all-zeros) source MAC address in the Ethernet header. Workaround: Avoid enabling DHCPv4 Snooping and DHCPv4 Relay simultaneously on the same VLAN.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.18.0001, what issue was resolved in PKI Bug 428981?
original_answer: PKI (Bug ID 428981): Symptom: EST-based certificate auto-enrollment and re-enrollment fails with an SSL connect error (CURL error 35) on AOS-CX 10.16 and later when the EST server requires TLS client certificate authentication and enforces it via TLS renegotiation. Scenario: This issue may occur when the EST client first retrieves CA certificates over an unauthenticated TLS connection and caches the session. When the server subsequently requests a client certificate by initiating TLS renegotiation on the reused session, the switch rejects the renegotiation, causing enrollment to fail. This behavior is observed with EST servers such as EJBCA when configured to require mutual TLS (mTLS) client certificate authentication. Workaround: Configure the EST server to accept password-based authentication (HTTP Digest or Basic) as an alternative to requiring TLS client certificate authentication on the enrollment endpoint, and configure username and password credentials in the AOS-CX EST profile. This changes the authentication method for EST enrollment.

This changes the authentication method for EST enrollment.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.18.0001, what issue was resolved in UDP Forwarder Bug 418074?
original_answer: UDP Forwarder (Bug ID 418074): Symptom: When ip forward-protocol udp is configured with a non-classful subnet broadcast address as the destination (for example, 10.232.104.127 for a /25 subnet), the forwarded packet is sent as unicast to that address instead of being broadcast on the destination subnet. Hosts on the destination subnet do not receive the forwarded UDP broadcast. Scenario: This issue occurs when the destination address configured in ip forward-protocol udp is a non-classful subnet broadcast address (not ending in.255). The UDP broadcast forwarder only recognizes classful subnet broadcast addresses (ending in.255) as broadcast destinations. Any other address, including valid subnet broadcast addresses for prefix lengths shorter than /24 (such as /25, /26, etc.), is treated as a unicast destination. The switch identifies a configured address as a broadcast destination solely based on whether the last octet is 255, regardless of the subnet prefix length. Workaround: Use only classful subnet broadcast addresses (ending in.255) as destinations in ip forward-protocol udp when broadcast delivery on the destination subnet is required. For non-/24 subnets where the broadcast address does not end in.255, configure each unicast destination host IP individually. A maximum of 8 forward-protocol entries are supported per interface.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.18.0001, under what scenario does UDP Forwarder Bug 418074 occur?
original_answer: UDP Forwarder (Bug ID 418074): This issue occurs when the destination address configured in ip forward-protocol udp is a non-classful subnet broadcast address (not ending in.255). The UDP broadcast forwarder only recognizes classful subnet broadcast addresses (ending in.255) as broadcast destinations. Any other address, including valid subnet broadcast addresses for prefix lengths shorter than /24 (such as /25, /26, etc.), is treated as a unicast destination. The switch identifies a configured address as a broadcast destination solely based on whether the last octet is 255, regardless of the subnet prefix length.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.18.0001, what is the workaround for UDP Forwarder Bug 418074?
original_answer: UDP Forwarder (Bug ID 418074): The documented workaround is: Use only classful subnet broadcast addresses (ending in.255) as destinations in ip forward-protocol udp when broadcast delivery on the destination subnet is required. For non-/24 subnets where the broadcast address does not end in.255, configure each unicast destination host IP individually. A maximum of 8 forward-protocol entries are supported per interface.

original_question: For 6000 AOS-CX 10.18.0001, what known issue is documented for DHCP Server Bug 420285?
original_answer: DHCP Server (Bug ID 420285): Symptom: DHCP clients connected via a relay agent either fail to obtain an IP address or receive an IP address from an incorrect DHCP pool instead of the pool intended for their subnet. Scenario: This issue occurs when the DHCP server has multiple pools configured with IP ranges that span non-default subnet boundaries (e.g., /23), and the prefix-len parameter is not explicitly specified in the range command. When a relay agent forwards client DISCOVER packets, the DHCP server uses the relay agent's gateway IP address (giaddr) to identify the matching pool. Without prefix-len, the server cannot determine the pool's subnet boundary and fails to correctly map the giaddr to the intended pool. Workaround: Explicitly specify prefix-len in the pool range configuration to match the intended subnet mask. For example: dhcp-server vrf default pool pool_name_ABC range 172.22.0.4 172.22.1.254 prefix-len <netmask>.
